beats.text: Guests don't say goodbye. They just... aren't there anymore. One by one the folding chairs fold themselves.
Dad sits alone at the picnic table with a cold hot dog. The porch light buzzes. Somewhere, a sprinkler starts. Saturday ends.
The squad cars pull away. The red-and-blue wash fades off the siding. The neighbors are still at their windows.
The cul-de-sac whispers. A blind lifts. A blind drops. A blind lifts again.
Karen won't look at him. She carries the potato salad inside. The screen door slaps once. Saturday ends.
Dad sinks into the patio chair. It creaks. He earned that creak.
The kid tears past, shrieking, chasing the dog, chasing nothing. The sun goes orange. Saturday ends — gentle, this time.
The sky goes lavender, then bruise, then dark. Someone is doing dishes. The water runs and runs.
Up and down the street, porch lights click on. Click off. Click on. Click off.
A dog barks twice and stops. Saturday ends.
